articles.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
articles.subtitle: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a
articles.author: Hayden Field
articles.summary: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form - granting access only to a small group of […]
body: The government will approve customer access on a case-by-case basis.
The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6.

The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form — granting access only to a small group of enterprise customers — in compliance with a request from the federal government. During that preview period, the Trump administration itself would reportedly approve access for customers on a case-by-case basis.

It’s a more favorable deal than the Trump administration gave OpenAI rival Anthropic, which earlier this month received an ultimatum requiring it to suspend access to its Mythos 5 and Fable 5, models. The administration issued an export control directive that prohibited “foreign nationals” from accessing the technology (including any of Anthropic’s own employees who were not US citizens). The seemingly heavy-handed approach to AI regulation, after promises from the Trump administration that it would take a “ speed wins ” approach to the technology and encourage an American AI exports program, raised alarm bells across the tech industry .
Now, some of those concerns seem to be coming to pass — and in a decidedly uneven way, depending on the company.
keyPoints: The government will approve customer access on a case-by-case basis.
The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6.

The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited preview form — granting access only to a small group of enterprise customers — in compliance with a request from the federal government. During that preview period, the Trump administration itself would reportedly approve access for customers on a case-by-case basis.
paragraphTranslations: 政府将逐个案例批准客户访问。
特朗普政府担心潜在的安全问题，据报道已要求OpenAI分阶段发布其下一个大型模型GPT-5.6。
《信息报》报道说，OpenAI首席执行官山姆·奥特曼周三在公司问答环节告诉员工，将发布GPT-5.6的有限预览版本——只授予一小部分企业客户访问权限——以遵守联邦政府的请求。在预览期间，特朗普政府本身将逐个案例批准客户访问。

与特朗普政府给予OpenAI竞争对手Anthropic的交易相比，这是一个更有利的协议，Anthropic本月早些时候收到了一个最后通牒，要求其暂停访问其Mythos 5和Fable 5模型。政府发布了一项出口控制指令，禁止“外国公民”访问该技术（包括Anthropic自己的非美国公民员工）。在特朗普政府承诺将采取“速度取胜”的方式来处理这项技术并鼓励美国AI出口计划之后，对AI监管的这种看似高压的做法，在科技行业引发了警报。
现在，这些担忧似乎正在以明显不平等的方式成为现实，这取决于公司。

paragraphs: The Trump administration has requested OpenAI to delay the release of its model GPT-5.6 due to security concerns. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that GPT-5.6 will be released in a limited preview, granting access only to a select group of enterprise customers. The government will approve customer access on a case-by-case basis during the preview period. This is a more favorable treatment compared to OpenAI's rival, Anthropic, which was forced to suspend access to its models Mythos 5 and Fable 5 for foreign nationals.
paragraphTranslations: 由于安全担忧，特朗普政府已要求OpenAI推迟其模型GPT-5.6的发布。OpenAI首席执行官萨姆·奥特曼宣布，GPT-5.6将以有限预览的形式发布，仅向选定的企业客户群体提供访问权限。在预览期间，政府将逐个案例批准客户访问权限。与OpenAI的竞争对手Anthropic相比，这是一种更优惠的待遇，后者被迫暂停了其模型Mythos 5和Fable 5对外国人的访问权限。

articles.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
articles.subtitle: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.
articles.author: Ashley Belanger
articles.summary: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.
body: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.

Anthropic has accused the Chinese firm Alibaba of launching the largest attack yet attempting to clone Claude, as China races to match the capabilities of Anthropic’s leading model following Mythos’ release and subsequent restriction from foreign markets .

Ars obtained a June 10 letter sent to Senators Tim Scott (R-SC) and Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) one day ahead of a Senate committee hearing on “AI and the American Dream.” In the letter, Anthropic shared “new, confidential evidence of the largest campaign to illicitly extract Claude’s capabilities we have ever measured.”

The attacks occurred between April 22 and June 5, when “operators afﬁliated with Alibaba and Alibaba Qwen, Alibaba’s AI lab” allegedly generated “more than 28.8 million exchanges with Claude through almost 25,000 fraudulent accounts,” Anthropic said. Violating Claude’s terms of service and access restrictions, this campaign “targeted some of Claude’s most valuable capabilities, such as agentic reasoning, software engineering, and long-horizon tasks.”

According to Anthropic, Alibaba evaded detection by “using obfuscation techniques and proxy networks.” As Chinese demand for reliable obfuscation techniques increases, Anthropic warned there’s already “a growing circumvention economy” to fuel an ever-expanding web of future distillation attacks.

Like other Chinese labs attempting to copy US frontier models, Alibaba’s aim, Anthropic alleged, was to extract Claude’s capabilities “without incurring the training and R&D costs required to train” their own frontier model. These attacks have become “widespread” and “turn hundreds of billions of dollars in American investment and R&D into a massive subsidy for our geopolitical competitors,” Anthropic said.

Importantly, Anthropic said, the Alibaba campaign occurred after Donald Trump took steps to curb such illicit distillation attacks and defend US national security. In April, Trump accused China of “industrial-scale” AI theft after Anthropic accused Chinese firms DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax of using the same tactic as Alibaba allegedly used to generate “over 16 million exchanges with Claude through approximately 24,000 fraudulent accounts.” OpenAI and Google have published findings on similar attacks on their models, Anthropic said.

Anthropic accused Alibaba of “brazenly” racing to make a copycat Claude, seemingly unfazed by Trump’s threats to crack down on foreign efforts to copy US frontier models despite depending on US investors.

“Alibaba is listed on the New York Stock Exchange, maintains business operations in the United States, and is accountable to US investors and regulators,” Anthropic’s letter noted, “yet this activity unfolded in the weeks after” Trump’s memo warned that cloning attempts were “unacceptable.”

Alibaba is already preparing to clash with Trump, though. In a lawsuit filed Tuesday, Alibaba accused the Trump administration of blacklisting the company after falsely linking the company to the Chinese military, Reuters reported . Alibaba is seeking to remove the Trump designation, which they claimed has “no basis in fact or law.”

“Alibaba is governed by an independent board, none of whom has any military affiliation,” Alibaba said. “Its products and services are built for retail, logistics, and enterprise information technology—not weapons, defense, or intelligence.”

Anthropic appears unconvinced, however, that Alibaba isn’t working with the Chinese government. In the letter, Anthropic warned that without stronger interventions, these distillation attacks will “help China reach Mythos Preview-level capabilities sooner.”
keyPoints: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.

Anthropic has accused the Chinese firm Alibaba of launching the largest attack yet attempting to clone Claude, as China races to match the capabilities of Anthropic’s leading model following Mythos’ release and subsequent restriction from foreign markets .

Ars obtained a June 10 letter sent to Senators Tim Scott (R-SC) and Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) one day ahead of a Senate committee hearing on “AI and the American Dream.” In the letter, Anthropic shared “new, confidential evidence of the largest campaign to illicitly extract Claude’s capabilities we have ever measured.”
paragraphTranslations: 阿里巴巴被指控使用25000个账户在超过2880万次交易中挖掘克劳德。
Anthropic公司指控这家中国公司阿里巴巴发动了迄今为止规模最大的攻击，试图克隆克劳德，因为中国正竞相匹配Anthropic公司领先模型的能力，继Mythos发布后以及随后对外国市场的限制。
Ars获得了一封6月10日发送给参议员Tim Scott（R-SC）和Elizabeth Warren（D-Mass.）的信，这封信是在参议院委员会就“AI与美国梦”举行听证会的前一天。在这封信中，Anthropic分享了“我们有史以来测量到的最大的非法提取克劳德能力的活动中的新、机密证据”。
这些攻击发生在4月22日至6月5日之间，当时“与阿里巴巴及其人工智能实验室阿里巴巴Qwen有关联的操作员”据称通过“近25000个欺诈性账户”与克劳德进行了“超过2880万次交易”，Anthropic说。违反了克劳德的服务条款和访问限制，这场活动“针对克劳德最有价值的一些能力，如代理推理、软件工程和长期任务”。
根据Anthropic的说法，阿里巴巴通过“使用混淆技术和代理网络”来逃避检测。随着对中国可靠的混淆技术需求的增加，Anthropic警告说，已经有一个“日益增长的规避经济”来推动未来分馏攻击的不断扩张的网络。
与其他试图复制美国前沿模型的中国实验室一样，Anthropic声称，阿里巴巴的目标是在不承担训练和研发自己的前沿模型所需的训练和研发成本的情况下提取克劳德的能力。这些攻击已经变得“普遍”，并将“数千亿美元的美国投资和研发转化为对我们地缘政治竞争对手的大规模补贴”，Anthropic说。

重要的是，Anthropic说，阿里巴巴的活动发生在唐纳德·特朗普采取措施遏制这种非法分馏攻击和捍卫美国国家安全之后。4月，特朗普在Anthropic指控中国公司DeepSeek、Moonshot和MiniMax使用与阿里巴巴据称用于通过大约24000个欺诈性账户与克劳德进行“超过1600万次交易”的相同战术后，指责中国进行“工业规模”的AI盗窃。Anthropic说，OpenAI和谷歌已经发表了关于对他们模型的类似攻击的研究成果。
Anthropic指控阿里巴巴“公然”急于制作一个克隆克劳德，似乎不受特朗普打击外国努力复制美国前沿模型的威胁的影响，尽管依赖于美国投资者。
“阿里巴巴在纽约证券交易所上市，在美国维持业务运营，并对美国投资者和监管机构负责，”Anthropic的信中指出，“然而，这些活动在”特朗普的备忘录警告克隆企图是“不可接受的”之后的几周内展开。
阿里巴巴已经在准备与特朗普对抗。据路透社报道，在周二提起的诉讼中，阿里巴巴指控特朗普政府错误地将公司与中国军方联系起来，将公司列入黑名单。阿里巴巴寻求撤销特朗普的指定，他们声称这“没有事实或法律依据”。
“阿里巴巴由一个独立的董事会管理，他们中没有人有任何军事隶属关系，”阿里巴巴说。“其产品和服务是为零售、物流和企业信息技术而构建的——不是武器、国防或情报。”
然而，Anthropic似乎不相信阿里巴巴没有与中国政府合作。在这封信中，Anthropic警告说，如果不进行更强有力的干预，这些分馏攻击将“帮助中国更快地达到Mythos预览级别的能力”。

paragraphs: Anthropic accuses Chinese firm Alibaba of launching the largest attack to clone Claude, Anthropic’s leading AI model. The company allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges, violating its terms of service. Anthropic warns of a growing circumvention economy to fuel future attacks. Alibaba’s aim is to extract Claude’s capabilities without incurring training and R&D costs. Anthropic says these attacks turn American investment into a subsidy for geopolitical competitors. Alibaba is challenging Trump's decision to blacklist the company, claiming it has no military affiliation.
paragraphTranslations: Anthropic指责中国公司阿里巴巴发动了规模最大的克隆Claude（Anthropic的领先AI模型）的攻击。该公司被指控使用2.5万个账户进行了超过2880万次的Claude挖掘，违反了其服务条款。Anthropic警告说，不断增长的规避经济将助长未来的攻击。阿里巴巴的目标是在不承担训练和研发成本的情况下提取Claude的能力。Anthropic表示，这些攻击将美国投资变成了地缘政治竞争对手的补贴。阿里巴巴正在挑战特朗普将公司列入黑名单的决定，声称其与军事无关。
